question_text: Two loudspeakers emit sound at different frequencies. Speaker A produces a high-pitched sound with a frequency of 4000 Hz, while Speaker B produces a low-pitched sound with a frequency of 250 Hz. Both speakers emit sound at the same amplitude. Explain which sound will have the longer wavelength and why, given that sound travels at 340 m/s in air.
correct_answer: Speaker B (250 Hz) will have the longer wavelength because wavelength is inversely proportional to frequency. Using wavelength = speed ÷ frequency: Speaker B has wavelength 340 ÷ 250 = 1.36 m, while Speaker A has 340 ÷ 4000 = 0.085 m.
explanation: The wave equation shows that as frequency decreases, wavelength increases (they are inversely related). Speaker B's lower frequency means its sound waves are stretched out more, creating a longer wavelength than Speaker A's high-frequency waves.
